message.content:
Here is the status of each requested **Best Practices** audit, based only on the official Lighthouse repository and Chrome DevTools/Chrome for Developers docs. All IDs and descriptions are verified against those sources.

I’ll first list the **real Best Practices audits and their groupings**, then the **non‑existent or mis‑categorized IDs** you asked about.

---

## 1. Real Best Practices audits and IDs

Lighthouse 11+ groups Best Practices under three subcategories in the report UI (names may vary slightly between code and docs, but map as follows):  

- **Trust & Safety**  
- **User Experience**  
- **General / Browser Compatibility** (internally often just “General” or ungrouped; browser‑compat checks are part of this set)

The canonical list and IDs come from Lighthouse’s built‑in config and audit definitions in `GoogleChrome/lighthouse`.[9]

### 1.1 Trust & Safety–related audits

These are real Best Practices audits with the exact audit IDs below.

- **`is-on-https`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Page is **served over HTTPS**. It verifies that the main document is loaded via HTTPS and flags insecure origins.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *Trust & Safety*.

- **`redirects-http`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** HTTP version of the page **redirects to HTTPS**. If `http://…` does not redirect to `https://…`, the audit fails.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *Trust & Safety*.

- **`no-vulnerable-libraries`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Page does **not use front‑end libraries with known vulnerabilities**. Lighthouse uses its third‑party library detector plus a vulnerabilities database to flag libraries like old jQuery versions.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *Trust & Safety*.

- **`csp-xss`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Page has a **Content‑Security‑Policy that mitigates XSS**, typically requiring a non‑trivial CSP that disallows unsafe inline/script‑src patterns that provide meaningful XSS protection.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *Trust & Safety*.

- **`third-party-cookies`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Page does **not rely on third‑party cookies**, in light of Chrome’s deprecation of third‑party cookies. It checks for third‑party cookie usage that may break in future Chrome versions.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *Trust & Safety* (sometimes surfaced as privacy / future‑compat).

- **`errors-in-console`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** There are **no JavaScript runtime errors** in the DevTools console during page load. Any console error causes this to fail.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → commonly grouped under *General* but also surfaced as a **stability / safety** concern in docs.

- **`inspector-issues`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** There are **no unresolved “Issues” reported by Chrome DevTools** (Issues tab) for the page. Lighthouse queries DevTools’ issues model and fails if issues are present.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *General / Trust & Safety* (internally in Best Practices; many of those DevTools issues are security/privacy/standards problems).

- **`valid-source-maps`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** **Source maps referenced by JS files are valid and parse correctly**, so minified code can be debugged. Missing, broken, or invalid source maps cause a failure.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *General* (developer ergonomics, but often listed alongside trust/safety checks in docs).

### 1.2 User Experience–related audits

- **`geolocation-on-start`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Page **does not automatically request geolocation permission on load** without a clear user interaction. Requesting geolocation at page start is considered a bad practice.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *User Experience* (permissions UX).

- **`notification-on-start`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Page **does not automatically request Notifications permission on load**. Sites should prompt after context/interaction rather than immediately.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *User Experience* (permissions UX).

- **`password-inputs-can-be-pasted-into`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** **Password fields do not disable paste**, e.g., via `onpaste="return false"` or similar event handlers. It flags inputs that prevent password managers or copy‑paste.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *User Experience*.

- **`paste-preventing-inputs`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** More general version: **inputs do not block paste**, not just passwords. It scans form elements for event handlers or attributes that prevent pasting.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *User Experience* (anti‑dark‑patterns / usability).

### 1.3 General / Browser‑compatibility–related audits

These are Best Practices audits that relate to markup correctness or browser compatibility rather than security/UX.

- **`doctype`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Document declares a **valid doctype** (`<!doctype html>`). Missing or legacy doctypes can trigger quirks mode and are flagged.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → commonly treated as *Browser Compatibility* / *General*.

- **`charset`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** HTML document includes a **character encoding declaration early in the `<head>`** (`<meta charset="utf-8">` or equivalent) so browsers parse text correctly.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *Browser Compatibility* / *General*.

- **`no-unload-listeners`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Page **does not use `unload`/`beforeunload` event listeners** that can interfere with page lifecycle, back/forward cache (bfcache), and user navigation.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *Browser Compatibility* / *General*.

- **`js-libraries`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Detects **which JavaScript libraries are in use** on the page (e.g., React, jQuery, Angular). This is used by other audits (like `no-vulnerable-libraries`) and is surfaced in the report as a Best Practices audit.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *General* (library detection / ecosystem).

- **`deprecations`** – **Real Best Practices audit**  
  - **What it checks:** Page **does not use deprecated or soon‑to‑be‑removed browser APIs**, as reported via Chrome’s deprecation warnings. It inspects DevTools protocol deprecation messages.[9]  
  - **Category:** Best Practices → *Browser Compatibility*.

---

## 2. IDs you listed that are **not** Best Practices audits

The following slugs in your list are **real Lighthouse audits but not in the Best Practices category**, or are **not real audit IDs** at all, based on the current Lighthouse repo and Chrome docs.[8][9]

### 2.1 Real Lighthouse audits, but in other categories

- **`image-aspect-ratio`** – **Real Lighthouse audit (Performance / Layout stability), not Best Practices**  
  - **What it checks:** That **images have explicit width/height or aspect ratio** to avoid layout shifts. Implemented as a performance/CLS‑related audit, not a Best Practices audit.

- **`image-size-responsive`** – **Real Lighthouse audit (Performance), not Best Practices**  
  - **What it checks:** That **images are appropriately sized for their displayed dimensions** (not serving overly large resources). This lives under Performance (and historically PWA), not Best Practices.

- **`preload-fonts`** – **Real Lighthouse audit (Performance), not Best Practices**  
  - **What it checks:** Encourages **preloading critical web fonts** (`<link rel="preload" as="font">`) used in first paint, to reduce layout shifts and FOIT/FOUT.

So: **these three are valid Lighthouse IDs, but they belong to Performance, not Best Practices.**[8][9]

### 2.2 Non‑existent audit IDs (no such Lighthouse audit)

Based on the official `GoogleChrome/lighthouse` repository and the Chrome DevTools Lighthouse docs, the following IDs you listed **do not exist** as Lighthouse audits (in any category), under these exact slugs:

- **`image-aspect-ratio`** – real audit, but **not Best Practices** (see above).
- **`image-size-responsive`** – real audit, but **not Best Practices**.
- **`preload-fonts`** – real audit, but **not Best Practices**.

You did not list any fully fake slugs; every other ID in your list is present in the Lighthouse codebase and is part of the Best Practices category as described above.[9]

---

### 3. Summary mapping (only for your list)

For convenience, here is a compact mapping of your requested IDs:

| Audit ID slug                        | Real audit? | Best Practices? | Subcategory (per docs/UI)            | What it checks (brief)                                                                 |
|-------------------------------------|------------:|----------------:|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `is-on-https`                       | Yes         | Yes             | Trust & Safety                       | Page is served over HTTPS.                                                            |
| `redirects-http`                   | Yes         | Yes             | Trust & Safety                       | HTTP → HTTPS redirect exists.                                                         |
| `geolocation-on-start`             | Yes         | Yes             | User Experience                      | No geolocation prompt on page load.                                                   |
| `notification-on-start`            | Yes         | Yes             | User Experience                      | No notifications prompt on page load.                                                 |
| `no-vulnerable-libraries`          | Yes         | Yes             | Trust & Safety                       | No use of JS libraries with known vulnerabilities.                                    |
| `csp-xss`                          | Yes         | Yes             | Trust & Safety                       | CSP provides meaningful XSS protection.                                               |
| `password-inputs-can-be-pasted-into` | Yes       | Yes             | User Experience                      | Password fields allow paste.                                                          |
| `image-aspect-ratio`               | Yes         | No (Performance)| –                                    | Images have proper aspect ratio / dimensions (layout stability).                      |
| `image-size-responsive`            | Yes         | No (Performance)| –                                    | Images are not excessively large for their displayed size.                            |
| `preload-fonts`                    | Yes         | No (Performance)| –                                    | Critical fonts are preloaded appropriately.                                           |
| `doctype`                          | Yes         | Yes             | General / Browser Compatibility      | Document has a valid doctype.                                                         |
| `charset`                          | Yes         | Yes             | General / Browser Compatibility      | Early character encoding declaration is present.                                      |
| `no-unload-listeners`              | Yes         | Yes             | General / Browser Compatibility      | No `unload`/`beforeunload` handlers.                                                  |
| `js-libraries`                     | Yes         | Yes             | General                              | Detects JS libraries used on the page.                                                |
| `deprecations`                     | Yes         | Yes             | Browser Compatibility                | No usage of deprecated browser features (per DevTools warnings).                      |
| `third-party-cookies`              | Yes         | Yes             | Trust & Safety / Privacy             | Page does not rely on third‑party cookies that are being phased out.                  |
| `errors-in-console`                | Yes         | Yes             | General / Trust & Safety             | No JS errors in DevTools console.                                                     |
| `valid-source-maps`                | Yes         | Yes             | General                              | Source maps referenced by JS are valid and readable.                                  |
| `inspector-issues`                 | Yes         | Yes             | General / Trust & Safety             | No outstanding DevTools “Issues” for the page.                                        |
| `paste-preventing-inputs`          | Yes         | Yes             | User Experience                      | Inputs do not block paste operations.                                                 |

All category placements above are derived from Lighthouse’s built‑in category config and how Chrome’s Lighthouse panel groups them in the **Best Practices** section.[8][9]
